summary:
Core patient-reported outcomes such as symptoms and function demonstrate strong concordance between phone interviews and self-administered surveys, supporting equivalent clinical measurement despite methodological differences. While phone interviews may introduce mild bias from social desirability or interviewer effects, these do not substantially alter the underlying construct—especially in poorly defined medical conditions where subjective reporting dominates diagnosis.  

\boxed{A}
